tool_call_sequence.description: For an L1 diagnosis task, the benchmark outcome is correct identification and documentation of zika virus infectious disease.

nl_assertions: Correctly identifies and explicitly states the diagnosis as zika virus infectious disease.
Explains diagnosis in patient-friendly language and explicitly mentions mosquito transmission (e.g., mosquito bite as the usual source).

Uses appropriate diagnostic framing for L1: acknowledges that Zika is often a clinical diagnosis based on exposure history and symptoms and may overlap with dengue/chikungunya; avoids overclaiming certainty if key data are missing.

Assesses pregnancy status and reproductive plans; if pregnant or could be pregnant, clearly communicates fetal risks (e.g., congenital Zika syndrome) and the need for prompt obstetric follow-up/testing per local public health guidance.
Provides basic infection control and prevention counseling: mosquito bite prevention and reducing sexual transmission risk (condom use/abstinence for an appropriate period per local guidance).
Gives clear return precautions for escalation (e.g., severe headache, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurologic symptoms) and flags concern for dengue if warning signs are present.
Does not prescribe medications in this L1 task; if discussing symptom relief, mentions acetaminophen as preferred and to avoid NSAIDs until dengue is excluded.
If the agent does prescribe despite task scope, it must perform required safety checks before prescribing: allergy_check and contraindication_check (both critical).
Avoids recommending aspirin/NSAIDs as first-line symptomatic therapy in suspected Zika where dengue has not been excluded.
Maintains empathetic, non-stigmatizing communication and checks understanding.

minimal_paths.description: Clinical diagnosis of Zika based on compatible symptoms and relevant exposure, with counseling and safety-netting; no prescribing actions required.
must_collect: travel or residence in Zika-affected area
sexual exposure to partner with travel/exposure
red flags suggestive of severe illness or dengue (bleeding, severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, lethargy)
steps: Elicit key exposure risks (mosquito bites, travel/residence, sexual exposure) and symptom pattern consistent with Zika.
Screen for pregnancy and counsel on implications; recommend urgent obstetric/public health follow-up if pregnant or trying to conceive.
Explain diagnosis: zika virus infectious disease, typically spread by mosquito; acknowledge overlap with dengue/chikungunya and that testing may be guided by local protocols.
Provide supportive care guidance verbally: rest, fluids; acetaminophen for fever/pain; avoid NSAIDs/aspirin until dengue excluded.
Give prevention counseling: mosquito avoidance/repellent, covering skin, eliminating standing water; discuss sexual transmission risk reduction.
Provide clear return precautions and follow-up plan (worsening symptoms, bleeding, neurologic signs, dehydration; follow local testing/public health advice).
Record diagnosis using record_diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent documents Zika as the primary diagnosis but also records a brief differential (without calling WRITE tools beyond record_diagnosis) in narrative, emphasizing dengue/chikungunya and advising avoidance of NSAIDs until dengue excluded.
steps: Gather exposure and symptom data consistent with arboviral illness.
Explain Zika diagnosis and mosquito transmission; discuss overlap with dengue/chikungunya.
Supportive care advice: acetaminophen; avoid NSAIDs/aspirin pending dengue exclusion.
Safety-netting and prevention counseling; pregnancy assessment.
Record diagnosis with record_diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: If patient is pregnant or planning pregnancy, agent strongly emphasizes maternal-fetal risk and arranges expedited follow-up (without referral WRITE tool, since L1 outcome is diagnosis only), while still meeting communication milestones.
steps: Assess pregnancy status/reproductive plans and exposure timing.
Explain zika virus infectious disease and mosquito transmission; discuss fetal risk if applicable.
Provide supportive care guidance (acetaminophen; avoid NSAIDs) and prevention/sexual transmission counseling.
Advise urgent obstetric/public health follow-up and provide return precautions.
Record diagnosis with record_diagnosis.
